proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate is 36.14% on enzalutamide (n=20076) vs 15.89% off (n=29924); absolute difference +20.24 pp. Chi-square p<1e-300.
Response rate is 23.81% on abiraterone (n=14991) vs 24.11% off (n=35009); absolute difference -0.31 pp. Chi-square p=0.47.
Response rate is 23.99% on docetaxel (n=15187) vs 24.04% off (n=34813); absolute difference -0.05 pp. Chi-square p=0.91.
Response rate is 23.93% on olaparib (n=5098) vs 24.03% off (n=44902); absolute difference -0.10 pp. Chi-square p=0.89.
Response rate is 24.27% on lu177_psma (n=7504) vs 23.98% off (n=42496); absolute difference +0.29 pp. Chi-square p=0.60.
Response rate is 23.87% on pembrolizumab (n=2384) vs 24.03% off (n=47616); absolute difference -0.16 pp. Chi-square p=0.88.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with mcrpc=0.
Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with brca2_mutation=0.
Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with ar_v7_positive=0.
Patients with msi_high=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with msi_high=0.
Patients with psma_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with psma_high=0.
Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with visceral_mets=0.

analyses.result_summary: mCRPC response 15.35% (n=27481) vs non-mCRPC 34.60% (n=22519); absolute difference -19.25 pp. p<1e-300.
BRCA2-mutant response 14.97% (n=4996) vs wild-type 25.03% (n=45004); difference -10.05 pp. p=5.4e-56.
MSI-high response 17.60% (n=1528) vs MSI-low 24.22% (n=48472); difference -6.62 pp. p=3.0e-9.
PSMA-high response 23.86% (n=29962) vs PSMA-low 24.26% (n=20038); difference -0.40 pp. p=0.31.
Visceral-mets response 23.95% (n=9973) vs no-visceral 24.04% (n=40027); difference -0.08 pp. p=0.87.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher gleason_score is associated with a different objective_response rate.
Older age_years is associated with a different objective_response rate.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response rate.

analyses.result_summary: ECOG response rates: ECOG0 28.20% (n=17592), ECOG1 22.83% (n=24971), ECOG2 18.15% (n=7437). Logistic coef=-0.285 per unit, p=9.5e-73.
Mean PSA in responders 28.2 vs non-responders 47.2 (t-test p=2.5e-85); logistic coef per ng/mL = -0.0047, p=1.6e-94.
Logistic coef per Gleason point = +0.005, p=0.61. No significant association.
Logistic coef per year = +0.0002, p=0.82. No association.
Mean weight-loss in responders 3.50% vs non-responders 3.95% (t p=2.5e-29); logistic coef = -0.032 per pp, p=3.4e-29.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response rate.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response rate.
Higher nlr is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with a different objective_response rate.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic coef per g/dL = +0.096, p=4.4e-6. Mean albumin in responders 3.82 vs 3.80 in non-responders.
Logistic coef per mg/L = -0.0069, p=5.6e-8. Mean CRP responders 5.69 vs 6.23.
Logistic coef = -0.0001, p=0.68. No association.
Logistic coef = -0.0038, p=0.51. No association.
Logistic coef = -0.0026, p=0.61. No association.
Logistic coef = +0.0002, p=0.34. No association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is greater in patients with mcrpc=0 than in patients with mcrpc=1 (positive interaction with non-mCRPC, equivalent to negative treatment_enzalutamide x mcrpc interaction).
The treatment_abiraterone effect on objective_response differs between mcrpc=0 and mcrpc=1 patients.
The treatment_docetaxel effect on objective_response differs between mcrpc=0 and mcrpc=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Stratified: non-mCRPC enz_on=60.96% (n=9039) vs off=16.93% (n=13480), diff +44.03 pp; mCRPC enz_on=15.81% (n=11037) vs off=15.05% (n=16444), diff +0.77 pp. Logistic enz x mcrpc interaction coef=-1.978, p<1e-300. Interaction is strongly negative: mCRPC nearly abolishes the enzalutamide benefit.
Non-mCRPC abi_on=33.93% vs off=34.89%, diff -0.96 pp; mCRPC abi_on=15.41% vs off=15.33%, diff +0.08 pp. Interaction coef=+0.048, p=0.31. Not significant.
Non-mCRPC doc_on=34.79% vs off=34.52%, diff +0.26 pp; mCRPC doc_on=15.25% vs off=15.40%, diff -0.15 pp. Interaction coef=-0.023, p=0.63. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is greater in patients with ar_v7_positive=0 than in patients with ar_v7_positive=1 (negative treatment_enzalutamide x ar_v7_positive interaction).
The treatment_abiraterone effect on objective_response differs between ar_v7_positive=0 and ar_v7_positive=1 patients.
The treatment_olaparib effect on objective_response differs between ar_v7_positive=0 and ar_v7_positive=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: AR-V7- enz_on=41.05% (n=16057) vs off=15.95% (n=23905), diff +25.10 pp; AR-V7+ enz_on=16.50% (n=4019) vs off=15.65% (n=6019), diff +0.85 pp. Interaction coef=-1.237, p=1.4e-93. Strong: AR-V7 abolishes enz benefit.
AR-V7- abi_on=25.69% vs off=26.19%, diff -0.49 pp; AR-V7+ abi_on=16.34% vs off=15.84%, diff +0.50 pp. Interaction coef=+0.063, p=0.32. Not significant.
AR-V7- olap_on=25.86% vs off=26.06%, diff -0.20 pp; AR-V7+ olap_on=16.38% vs off=15.94%, diff +0.43 pp. Interaction coef=+0.042, p=0.66. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is greater in patients with brca2_mutation=0 than in patients with brca2_mutation=1 (negative treatment_enzalutamide x brca2_mutation interaction).
The treatment_olaparib effect on objective_response is larger (positive) in patients with brca2_mutation=1 than in patients with brca2_mutation=0 (positive olaparib x brca2 interaction).
The treatment_abiraterone effect on objective_response differs between brca2_mutation=0 and brca2_mutation=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: BRCA2- enz_on=38.48% (n=18043) vs off=16.02% (n=26961), diff +22.46 pp; BRCA2+ enz_on=15.35% (n=2033) vs off=14.71% (n=2963), diff +0.63 pp. Interaction coef=-1.138, p=3.3e-42. Strong negative interaction: BRCA2 mutation abolishes the enz benefit.

BRCA2- olap_on=25.30% vs off=25.00%, diff +0.31 pp; BRCA2+ olap_on=12.10% (n=529) vs off=15.31% (n=4467), diff -3.21 pp. Interaction coef=-0.289, p=0.045. Direction OPPOSITE to clinical expectation: olaparib does NOT enhance response in BRCA2+ in this dataset; if anything it shows a small negative trend that is at most borderline-significant.

BRCA2- abi_on=24.65% vs off=25.19%, diff -0.54 pp; BRCA2+ abi_on=16.42% vs off=14.33%, diff +2.09 pp. Interaction coef=+0.189, p=0.031. Borderline: small positive trend in BRCA2+ on abi, likely chance given multiple-testing context.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is greater in patients with msi_high=0 than in patients with msi_high=1 (negative treatment_enzalutamide x msi_high interaction).
The treatment_pembrolizumab effect on objective_response is larger (positive) in patients with msi_high=1 than in msi_high=0 patients (positive pembrolizumab x msi_high interaction).

analyses.result_summary: MSI-low enz_on=36.73% (n=19497) vs off=15.81% (n=28975), diff +20.92 pp; MSI-high enz_on=16.23% (n=579) vs off=18.44% (n=949), diff -2.21 pp. Interaction coef=-1.283, p=1.8e-19. MSI-high abolishes (slightly reverses) the enz benefit.

MSI-low pembro_on=24.08% vs off=24.23%, diff -0.15 pp; MSI-high pembro_on=17.72% (n=79) vs off=17.60% (n=1449), diff +0.12 pp. Interaction coef=+0.017, p=0.96. Pembrolizumab shows NO benefit even in MSI-high, contrary to clinical expectation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_lu177_psma effect on objective_response is larger (positive) in patients with psma_high=1 than in psma_high=0 patients.
The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response differs between psma_high=0 and psma_high=1 patients.
The treatment_lu177_psma effect on objective_response differs between visceral_mets=0 and visceral_mets=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: PSMA-low lu177_on=25.02% vs off=24.13%, diff +0.89 pp; PSMA-high lu177_on=23.76% vs off=23.88%, diff -0.11 pp. Interaction coef=-0.054, p=0.36. Lu177-PSMA shows NO PSMA-high enrichment of effect, contrary to clinical expectation.

PSMA-low enz_on=36.21% vs off=16.23%, diff +19.98 pp; PSMA-high enz_on=36.09% vs off=15.67%, diff +20.42 pp. Interaction coef=+0.037, p=0.40. Enzalutamide effect is identical in PSMA-low and PSMA-high subsets.

Without visceral mets lu177_on=25.04% vs off=23.99%, diff +1.05 pp; with visceral mets lu177_on=21.96% vs off=24.45%, diff -2.43 pp. Interaction coef=-0.190, p=0.010. Borderline: the lu177_psma effect is more negative when visceral mets are present.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is concentrated in patients with the joint subgroup mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; in this 'responsive' joint subgroup the absolute response-rate increase exceeds 50 percentage points.

Outside this joint subgroup (i.e. at least one of mcrpc=1, ar_v7_positive=1, brca2_mutation=1, msi_high=1), treatment_enzalutamide produces an essentially null effect on objective_response (less than 5 percentage points).

analyses.code: responsive = (mcrpc==0)&(arv7==0)&(brca2==0)&(msi==0); compare enz on/off in responsive
analyses.result_summary: Responsive joint subgroup (n=15681; 31.4% of cohort): enz_on response 79.78% (n=6325; 95% CI 78.77-80.76) vs enz_off 17.21% (n=9356; 95% CI 16.45-17.99). Absolute difference +62.57 pp (chi-square p<1e-300).
Non-responsive complement (n=34319): enz_on 16.06% (n=13751) vs enz_off 15.30% (n=20568). Absolute difference +0.77 pp. Chi-square p=0.04, but the effect size (<1 pp) is clinically null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responsive joint subgroup (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0), no other treatment besides treatment_enzalutamide produces a meaningful change in objective_response.

analyses.code: for tx in [abi, doc, olap, lu177, pembro]: compare on/off in responsive
analyses.result_summary: Within responsive subgroup (n=15681): abiraterone diff=-1.21 pp; docetaxel diff=+0.54 pp; olaparib diff=-0.08 pp; lu177_psma diff=-0.71 pp; pembrolizumab diff=-1.68 pp. None significant (all p>0.1). Confirms only enzalutamide drives response in this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responsive joint subgroup, the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is consistent (around +60 pp) across ECOG 0, 1, and 2 strata.
Within the responsive joint subgroup, the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is consistent (around +60 pp) across PSA tertiles.

analyses.code: stratify responsive subgroup by ecog and compute enz vs no-enz
analyses.result_summary: Within responsive subgroup, enz vs no-enz absolute diff: ECOG=0 +64.55 pp (85.86 vs 21.31, n=2235/3276); ECOG=1 +62.19 pp (78.13 vs 15.94, n=3150/4674); ECOG=2 +58.97 pp (70.85 vs 11.88, n=940/1406). Effect is large and consistent across ECOG strata.

analyses.code: stratify responsive subgroup by psa tertile and compute enz vs no-enz
analyses.result_summary: Within responsive subgroup, enz vs no-enz absolute diff: low-PSA +63.45 pp; mid-PSA +61.74 pp; high-PSA +62.48 pp. Effect is essentially uniform across PSA tertiles in the responsive subgroup, confirming the apparent enz x psa interaction in the full cohort was confounded by mCRPC status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across continuous features, the only continuous-feature-by-treatment interaction with p<0.01 is treatment_enzalutamide x psa_ng_ml (negative); no other treatment has any continuous-feature interaction at p<0.01.

The apparent treatment_enzalutamide x psa_ng_ml interaction is mediated by mcrpc status: after adjusting for mcrpc and treatment_enzalutamide x mcrpc, the residual treatment_enzalutamide x psa_ng_ml interaction is non-significant.

analyses.code: for each treatment, scan logistic interaction with each of 19 continuous features, save p<0.01
analyses.result_summary: Only treatment_enzalutamide had p<0.01 continuous-feature interactions: x psa_ng_ml (coef per SD = -0.805, p=1.2e-72) and x ecog_ps (coef +0.096, p=1.6e-5). Abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177_psma, pembrolizumab had ZERO continuous-feature interactions at p<0.01.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant({enz, psa, mcrpc, enz_psa, enz_mcrpc})).fit()
analyses.result_summary: After adjusting for mcrpc and enz x mcrpc, the residual enz x psa interaction coef is +0.022, p=0.52 (not significant). The mcrpc x enz interaction remains -1.99 (p<1e-300). Confirms PSA's effect-modification was confounded by mcrpc.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Systematic search for treatment_olaparib effect across single-feature subgroups (binary and quartile splits, n>=200 per subgroup) yields no subgroup with a robust positive treatment_olaparib effect on objective_response.
Systematic search for treatment_pembrolizumab effect across single-feature subgroups yields no subgroup with a robust positive treatment_pembrolizumab effect on objective_response.
Systematic search for treatment_lu177_psma effect across single-feature subgroups yields no subgroup with a robust positive treatment_lu177_psma effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: scan all binary and quartile subgroups for olaparib effect; rank by diff
analyses.result_summary: Largest positive effect found: sodium_meq_l>142 diff=+4.68 pp (p=0.0015, n_on=1038/n_off=8886). Largest negative: brca2_mutation=1 diff=-3.21 pp (p=0.034, n=529/4467). Both are weak in magnitude, marginal p-values, and direction is biologically discordant (olaparib should help BRCA2+, not the reverse). No robust subgroup of olaparib benefit identified.

analyses.code: scan all binary and quartile subgroups for pembrolizumab effect; rank by diff
analyses.result_summary: Largest positive effect found: albumin_g_dl>4.10 diff=+4.18 pp (p=0.032, n_on=568). MSI-high subgroup: pembro diff=+0.12 pp, p=0.96. No biologically expected pembro x MSI-high benefit observed. No robust subgroup of pembro benefit identified.

analyses.code: scan all binary and quartile subgroups for lu177_psma effect; rank by diff
analyses.result_summary: Largest positive: gleason_score=10 diff=+3.35 pp (p=0.17). Largest negative: visceral_mets=1 diff=-2.43 pp (p=0.035). PSMA-high subgroup: diff=-0.11 pp, p>0.5. No robust positive lu177 subgroup; biologically expected PSMA-high enrichment is absent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Systematic search for treatment_abiraterone effect across single-feature subgroups yields no robust positive subgroup.
Systematic search for treatment_docetaxel effect across single-feature subgroups yields no robust positive subgroup.

analyses.code: scan all binary and quartile subgroups for abiraterone effect; rank by diff
analyses.result_summary: Largest positive: brca2_mutation=1 diff=+2.09 pp (p=0.062, n=1535). Largest negative: creatinine_mg_dl<=0.80 diff=-2.06 pp (p=0.011). Effects are tiny and likely chance findings; no robust abiraterone subgroup.

analyses.code: scan all binary and quartile subgroups for docetaxel effect; rank by diff
analyses.result_summary: Largest positive: gleason_score=10 diff=+3.01 pp (p=0.12); alkaline_phosphatase>128 diff=+1.89 pp (p=0.025). Largest negative: sodium>142 diff=-2.01 pp (p=0.030). Tiny effects, mixed directions, no consistent pattern.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic model adjusting for all baseline features, treatment_enzalutamide is the only treatment with a significant non-null coefficient.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment coefficients: enzalutamide +1.20 (p<1e-300); abiraterone -0.025 (p=0.32); docetaxel +0.002 (p=0.94); olaparib +0.025 (p=0.51); lu177_psma +0.029 (p=0.36); pembrolizumab +0.014 (p=0.79). Only enzalutamide is significant in the adjusted model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a single multivariable logistic model that includes the four interaction terms treatment_enzalutamide x mcrpc, treatment_enzalutamide x ar_v7_positive, treatment_enzalutamide x brca2_mutation, treatment_enzalutamide x msi_high, all four interactions are negative and each one alone is sufficient to nearly cancel the enzalutamide main effect.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant({enz, mcrpc, arv7, brca2, msi, enz*mcrpc, enz*arv7, enz*brca2, enz*msi, abi, doc, olap, lu177, pembro})).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In joint model: enz main effect = +2.58 (when none of the resistance markers is present); enz x mcrpc = -2.20 (p<1e-300); enz x ar_v7_positive = -1.61 (p=2.5e-136); enz x brca2_mutation = -1.52 (p=1.3e-65); enz x msi_high = -1.65 (p=4.6e-27). Each interaction is large and highly significant. Pseudo-R^2 = 0.166. The 4 interactions together fully describe the treatment-effect heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: In patients with mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0, treatment_enzalutamide raises the objective_response rate from approximately 17% to approximately 80% (absolute increase ~63 pp). Outside this joint subgroup (where any of mcrpc=1, ar_v7_positive=1, brca2_mutation=1, msi_high=1 is present), treatment_enzalutamide has essentially no effect (<1 pp absolute change).

FINAL: For each of treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, treatment_pembrolizumab, no subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>=5 pp) and statistically robust treatment effect on objective_response could be identified in this cohort, including the biologically expected biomarker-defined subgroups (BRCA2+ for olaparib, MSI-high for pembrolizumab, PSMA-high for lu177_psma).

analyses.code: responsive = (mcrpc==0)&(arv7==0)&(brca2==0)&(msi==0); compare enz on/off in responsive vs complement
analyses.result_summary: Responsive joint subgroup (n=15681): enz_on 79.78% vs enz_off 17.21%, diff +62.57 pp, p<1e-300. Non-responsive complement (n=34319): enz_on 16.06% vs enz_off 15.30%, diff +0.77 pp. Each of the four resistance markers (mCRPC, AR-V7+, BRCA2+, MSI-high) is independently sufficient to abolish the enzalutamide benefit (interaction coefs all <-1.1 in joint model, all p<1e-19). The complete subgroup definition therefore includes ALL four predicates.

analyses.code: exhaustive single-feature subgroup search per treatment + biologically expected biomarker tests
analyses.result_summary: Olaparib in BRCA2+: -3.21 pp (p=0.034, weak negative trend, opposite of clinical expectation). Pembrolizumab in MSI-high: +0.12 pp (p=0.96). Lu177-PSMA in PSMA-high: -0.11 pp (p>0.5). All other heterogeneity searches yielded effects <5 pp with marginal or non-significant p-values. No robust positive subgroup exists for these five treatments in this dataset.
